Profile.title: Profile
Profile.description: Represents the authenticated user profile mirrored from Supabase Auth into the public schema.

email.description: Primary email address for the user.

displayName.description: Preferred display name shown in the UI.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task to be managed, containing details such as name, description, due date, priority, and workflow status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

subDetails.description: Additional details or a description of the task.

dueDate.description: The date when the task is due.

reminderTime.description: The time to remind the user about the task.

priority.description: The priority of the task (e.g., HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW).

category.description: Optional task category used for filtering and reporting.

wasCarriedForward.description: Indicates if the task was automatically carried forward from a previous day.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the task was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the task was last updated.

Meeting.title: Meeting
Meeting.description: Represents a scheduled meeting with details such as title, scheduling data, meeting notes, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Meeting entity.

subtitle.description: A subtitle or description for the meeting.

dateTime.description: The date and time when the meeting is scheduled.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the meeting was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the meeting was last updated.

tables.name: profiles
tables.description: Public profile mirror keyed by auth user UUID.

tables.name: tasks
tables.description: User-owned task records secured by row level security on user_id.

tables.name: meetings
tables.description: User-owned meeting records secured by row level security on user_id.

supabase.reasoning: The Supabase schema uses row level security with ownership policies on profiles, tasks, and meetings so every query is scoped by auth.uid(). Tasks and meetings are normalized into top-level relational tables with indexes on user_id plus the primary sorting fields, which improves dashboard analytics, list views, and long-term maintainability. This structure supports SQL reporting, simpler migrations, and production-grade access control without embedding authorization rules in document paths.
